title: 3D Scroll, Anchor, text-box, Repulsion & Liquid Tabs
description: Five fresh UI patterns: CSS scroll-driven 3D gallery, anchor positioning, text-box-trim precision, particle repulsion field, and spring-morph liquid tabs.

drops.title: 3D Scroll-Driven Gallery
drops.description: Cards rotate through 3D space as they pass the viewport — driven by animation-timeline: view(). Zero JS.
drops.concept: Uses the CSS Scroll-Driven Animations API's view() timeline to drive rotateY and translateX transforms on each card as it enters and exits the viewport. Alternate cards flip the opposite direction for variety. Entirely declarative CSS — no JavaScript animation at all.
drops.source: Codrops — Exploring 3D Image Rotations on Scroll (June 2026)

interaction.mouse: Scroll page to see cards rotate in 3D

drops.title: CSS Anchor Positioning
drops.description: Tooltips, popups, and badges anchored to elements using the new CSS anchor() function — no JS.
drops.concept: CSS Anchor Positioning (Chrome 125+, now broadly supported) lets you position floating elements relative to arbitrary anchors without JavaScript. This demo shows three scenarios: button tooltips, team avatar profile cards, and architecture diagram labels — all positioned purely in CSS using position-anchor and anchor().
drops.source: Chrome Developers — CSS Anchor Positioning API

interaction.mouse: Hover buttons and avatars to reveal anchored popups

drops.title: text-box-trim Precision
drops.description: The new text-box: trim-both cap alphabetic rule eliminates half-leading for perfect optical alignment.
drops.concept: CSS text-box-trim (Chrome 133+, Safari 18.2+) removes the half-leading above cap-height and below the alphabetic baseline, letting text elements align pixel-perfectly without margin hacks. This demo shows a toggle between trimmed and untrimmed across six real-world use cases: buttons, badges, headings, icon lists, hero text, and form labels.

interaction.click: Toggle the switch to compare trimmed vs untrimmed text

drops.title: Particle Repulsion Field
drops.description: Hover to trigger spring-physics repulsion — dots flee the cursor and snap back elastically.
drops.concept: A grid of ~800 particles on a canvas that respond to cursor proximity with spring-physics repulsion (or attraction mode). Each particle stores origin coordinates and springs back using per-frame velocity integration with damping. Three visual modes (dots with velocity elongation, grid squares, rings) plus sliders for repulsion radius, force strength, and damping. Inspired by the physics approach from Codrops' gravity trail experiment.
drops.source: Codrops — Made With GSAP: Gravity-Based Mouse Trail (May 2026)

interaction.mouse: Move cursor over canvas to repel particles
interaction.click: Switch between Dots/Grid/Rings modes or toggle Attract

drops.title: Liquid Morphing Tabs
drops.description: Tab indicator stretches and morphs between positions via spring-physics FLIP — swap easing live.
drops.concept: Instead of CSS width/left transitions (which can look mechanical), this uses a spring-physics FLIP animator that reads getBoundingClientRect() positions and drives an independent animation loop. The indicator rubber-bands and overshoots realistically. Four variants are shown: pill tabs (stretch-morph), underline tabs (gradient sweep), icon tabs (glow morph), and a segmented control. A live easing selector switches between Spring, Bounce, Ease, and Linear physics presets.

interaction.click: Click tabs to see liquid indicator morph; switch easing preset
